creator: schoutm@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Roryan from comment #32)

Not sure why these comments were added to this bug, but the current version of the port is 2.54.2, and all of the issues mentioned here do not apply any longer.

Specifically:

- liblz4 is non-optional at this point.  Upstream changed this to a requirement.
- python is in the USES line.
- SFTP and ZSTD compile flags are toggled via SFTP_MESON_(ON|OFF) / ZSTD_MESON_(ON|OFF).  I worked with upstream to ensure that libssh2 and libzstd are explicitly NOT enabled if configured this way.
